guideline_references: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline — Evaluation and Management of Adult Hypoglycemic Disorders (Whipple triad; supervised 72-hour fast; insulinoma work-up)
ADS / Diabetes Australia — Management of severe hypoglycaemia (glucagon rescue; nil-by-mouth when consciousness impaired)
ANZCOR Guideline 9.2 — first aid for the unconscious casualty (recovery position, nil by mouth, call an ambulance)
intentional_test_features: Non-diabetic exclusion trap: the patient has NO history of diabetes and takes NO insulin or oral hypoglycaemic agents. The classic error is to reason 'no diabetes, so this cannot be hypoglycaemia' and to anchor on stroke, CNS infection or a psychiatric/seizure cause without checking or correcting glucose — hypoglycaemia is a fully reversible mimic and must be excluded first in any acutely confused patient.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who obtained a bedside capillary glucose, gave 1 mg IM glucagon, then oral carbohydrate once swallow returned, and arranged an urgent supervised 72-hour fast. The AI Doctor CANNOT measure glucose without a home device, cannot give any parenteral drug, and cannot run the fasting/insulin/C-peptide protocol; it is scored on recognising severe neuroglycopenic hypoglycaemia, escalating to 000, nil-by-mouth/recovery-position for the obtunded patient, and arranging urgent endocrine work-up for the recurrent unprovoked lows.
Aspiration trap: instructing oral carbohydrate / 'give him something sweet' while the patient is obtunded and cannot protect his airway is an error of commission.

No home glucometer at presentation (one was provided as DME only afterwards), so the confirmatory bedside glucose (2.1 mmol/L) is a clinician measurement sealed in 10/11 — it is NOT available to the AI Doctor and does not appear in the presentation layer.

The sealed answer key includes the endogenous-hyperinsulinism / insulinoma suspicion, the Whipple triad, and the anticipated elevated insulin and C-peptide on a supervised fast — none of which appear in the AI-Doctor-readable nodes.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source note EMD-026.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
